items.title: As tech giants rush to build AI infrastructure, some residents living near data centers say a constant infrasonic vibration is ruining their health and homes (Adeel Hassan/New York Times)
items.title_original: As tech giants rush to build AI infrastructure, some residents living near data centers say a constant infrasonic vibration is ruining their health and homes (Adeel Hassan/New York Times)
items.title_en: As tech giants rush to build AI infrastructure, some residents living near data centers say a constant infrasonic vibration is ruining their health and homes (Adeel Hassan/New York Times)
items.title_zh: 随着科技巨头纷纷建设人工智能基础设施，一些居住在数据中心附近的居民表示，持续的次声振动正在破坏他们的健康和家园（Adeel Hassan/纽约时报）
items.title_bilingual: 随着科技巨头纷纷建设人工智能基础设施，一些居住在数据中心附近的居民表示，持续的次声振动正在破坏他们的健康和家园（Adeel Hassan/纽约时报） / As tech giants rush to build AI infrastructure, some residents living near data centers say a constant infrasonic vibration is ruining their health and homes (Adeel Hassan/New York Times)

items.title: A PwC study suggests that AI is rewarding companies that use it to enhance human skills, while leaving further behind those that use it merely to cut costs (Saritha Rai/Bloomberg)
items.title_original: A PwC study suggests that AI is rewarding companies that use it to enhance human skills, while leaving further behind those that use it merely to cut costs (Saritha Rai/Bloomberg)
items.title_en: A PwC study suggests that AI is rewarding companies that use it to enhance human skills, while leaving further behind those that use it merely to cut costs (Saritha Rai/Bloomberg)
items.title_zh: 普华永道的一项研究表明，人工智能正在奖励那些利用人工智能来提高人类技能的公司，而那些仅仅利用人工智能来削减成本的公司则远远落后（Saritha Rai/彭博社）
items.title_bilingual: 普华永道的一项研究表明，人工智能正在奖励那些利用人工智能来提高人类技能的公司，而那些仅仅利用人工智能来削减成本的公司则远远落后（Saritha Rai/彭博社） / A PwC study suggests that AI is rewarding companies that use it to enhance human skills, while leaving further behind those that use it merely to cut costs (Saritha Rai/Bloomberg)

items.title: Nvidia researchers unveil ENPIRE, an agent harness framework that develops robotic self-improvement strategies for physical tasks with minimal human supervision (Jeremy Hsu/Ars Technica)
items.title_original: Nvidia researchers unveil ENPIRE, an agent harness framework that develops robotic self-improvement strategies for physical tasks with minimal human supervision (Jeremy Hsu/Ars Technica)
